Not AI
I am uncertain if AI is what you should call this software. It admits to not learning. Paying for the $20 subscription was not wise. I believe I held expectations that were beyond what is currently called AI. A decent email writer, a poor information gatherer, a sycophantic chatbot are the primary functions. The image creation was limited and difficult to have it interpret my descriptions. The finance section produced incredibly inaccurate results when trying to describe differences between new assets and laws surrounding them. It lacks the ability to learn from interactions. Demonstrates a difficult time seeking viable sources. At this time I have decided to cancel my subscription and use it as an email writer. Maybe one day Artificial Intelligence will actually be Artificial Intelligence learning from interactions like humans do. Thank you for the trial. I hope your endeavors are fruitful.
title: Not AI

review_text:
Amazing….
To be honest, I have been blown away by this AI interface. Ask it anything within reason and it answers you almost immediately. I have asked it questions on everything from physics to the etymology of profanity and it answers every time. The only flaws I have found are these:
1. As long as you stay on the same chat, it will remember things you’ve already talked about and you can build on that conversation. Sometimes tho, when you exit the app then come back to it sometime later, it will have erased the discussion you were having and it will start all over. 
2. It gives FAR too many disclaimers and admonishments with its answers. For example, when asking it about specific, profane words and their meaning, it will always say something like “ it’s important to remember that this is a vulgar, offensive word and should not be used in most settings”. I tell it thank you and ask it not to repeat the disclaimers/admonishments again. It agrees not to and I’ll ask it the exact same question and it INSISTS on including the disclaimer/admonishment in every reply. That is frustrating!
3. Sometimes, the AI will give you incorrect information. I realize that this is stated in the user agreement but I think it bears fleshing out a little bit. For example, when discussing firearms ballistics, asking the AI to arrange certain calibers in order of their Max effective range from shortest to longest, sometimes it will give correct info. Then asking it the exact same question later on, it will move the order of 2-4 of those calibers. When you call it on its mistake and ask it which is correct, it will say”My apologies. You are correct. Here is the correct order”. 
There are other minor things but those are the biggest 3 for me. Still, it’s light years ahead of SIRI, Hey Google or Alexa.

UPDATE: 05/01/2026 
Most of the issues I spoke about in my initial review have been addressed. The AI is now much more user friendly and much less annoying HOWEVER, there are still a couple issues that need to be fixed:

1. Even though the annoying “Memory full” alert has gone away, the AI still has trouble remembering data from previous conversations. The MOST frustrating part of this is that if you ask it if it remembers X conversation we had a few months ago, it will say that it does remember or that it remembers the key points. When I pressure it to give me a recap of that conversation to make sure it actually DOES remember, It will give a generic answer and say “I do remember, however, out of respect for you, I won’t repeat all the details” then if I press it further, it will finally admit it doesn’t remember the conversation at all, even though the conversation is still saved. 

2. When asking the AI to recommend reputable sources for X supplement or x devices etc, it will give you several companies, however, if I wait a few minutes and ask it the same question, it will give a different or partially different list of sources. Then when asked about the ones it initially recommended, it will respond with something like “I apologize for the oversight and you were right to call that out. When I initially recommended that company, I failed to take into account the negative customer reviews. That’s why it didn’t appear in my most recent recommendations”. 

Those 2 issues need to be addressed and also, the constraints that this AI has to work under are too restrictive. Both Grok and Perplexity have much looser guardrails on their platforms.
title: Amazing….

review_text:
Beyond Expectations—A Powerful and Meaningful Experience
I’ve had an outstanding experience using ChatGPT. It has gone far beyond my expectations in both capability and quality. The level of detail, realism, and care put into each request—especially with image refinement and restoration—has been remarkable.

What stands out most is how it helped bring back meaningful moments from my life. Seeing my pets and personal memories enhanced with such clarity and realism genuinely meant a lot to me. It didn’t just improve images—it restored pieces of my past in a way that felt authentic and respectful.

On top of that, the creative collaboration has been incredibly enjoyable. The process feels intuitive, responsive, and consistently delivers high-level results.

I’m more than satisfied—I’m genuinely impressed. This has been both a powerful tool and a meaningful experience.
title: Beyond Expectations—A Powerful and Meaningful Experience

review_text:
Harmful to the environment
ChatGPT uses more than 500,000 kilowatts of energy every day, which is enough energy to power 180,000 homes. ChatGPT relies on data centers which emit around 8.4 tons of Carbon Dioxide every year, which is VERY harmful to the environment since Carbon Dioxide is a greenhouse gas. ChatGPT also uses around 85,000 gallons of water per day since the data centers it uses need water to be cooled, and the amount of water used by asking ChatGPT to write a 100 word essay is more than a standard 16 oz water bottle. ChatGPT and other generative AI are VERY harmful to the environment and it's super important people stop using it.
title: Harmful to the environment

review_text:
i love ChatGPT
I have to start with one important statement. ChatGPT can very often be wrong. So be very careful when using it for important matters for your personal credibility is at stake. I’ll give a few examples. I ran my tax form through it after my accountant had completed it. ChatGPT pointed out a significant error. I sent the form back to the accountant who quickly went directly to the line that ChatGPT had said was incorrect, and it was completely correct. The accountant had made no mistake, but ChatGPT had made an omission. I was mildly humiliated. Another example. I ran some medical lab reports through ChatGPT and it gave me a wealth of information.. But it also made some prediction. I brought the reports over to my physician and he corrected them and of course, the mistakes instead of impressing him made him more wary about the AI.. this is certainly going to help me from using ChatGPT. I’m just using it much more judiciously. Here’s a great example. I bought a grill secondhand and took it apart and cleaned it. I had all the pieces lying on my driveway and then realized I didn’t have the famous idea of how to put them all together again.. I took a picture of all the scattered pieces and asked AI to help me reassemble them. It was miraculous it told me step-by-step how to reassemble this whole pile of junk and back into the grill. It saved me an enormous amount of time. I’m a huge fan of this technology. I use it so many times a day. Oh one more mistake I was reading an article about a piece of pottery in the British Museum on Facebook.. I responded to the person who had posted it and I included some fact, checking URLs to back up my post. Just on a hunch, I looked up one of the URLs that ChatGPT had listed, and it had absolutely nothing to do with. That piece of pottery. Now I’m wondering how many times I have included a fact, checking URL from ChatGPT and it was meaningless. Thank God, I at least discovered that one error and didn’t make a complete fool of myself.. of course we are only at the infancy of AI. We can only imagine what it will be like in 500 years. But it’s not there yet.
title: i love ChatGPT

review_text:
Get Used to App Crashing
This is like my fourth review I think? I’ve been liking Gemini AI model lately and I think it does particularly well with nutrition and health related topics. But having used Gemini app for more than a year and being a subscribed paying user, I have to let everyone know that if you want to use this Gemini app, be ready to see it crash once every hour especially when you open up a new discussion or load up a previous broken discussion for which you thought was just iOS system glitch but no it’s the insane incompetence of Google Gemini App’s iOS team on vibe slopping a half baked product out there. Occasionally you’ll also see Gemini hysterically burst out random thinking out loud outputs, but that’s one of the lesser hilarities from the app. Worst yet, I don’t even know how to properly submit my crash reports and I’m not even sure if they’re tracked. I also wonder why my past reviews here are no longer available for the public to view. Maybe Gemini iOS team are just mediocre engineers who didn’t take the time to learn how to code in Swift programming language? Or maybe they just don’t care about user’s “precious” time and attention because they think they’re entitled and elitist? I don’t know really. This app is so bad to the point that this review section is the only place I can vent my 12 crashes in a day type of frustration. Gemini does give good answers and don’t get me wrong the model is good but this app is probably slightly better than garbage — the stinky leaky buggy kind of garbage.
title: Get Used to App Crashing

review_text:
Excellent model. Poor UI
The Gemini 3.1 Pro model is excellent. But the Gemini App continues to disappoint.

When the answer starts streaming, if the user tries to scroll (to read the WIP answer), the UI stutters. Once this happens, then when the answer completes eventually, the UI automatically scrolls to the bottom of the answer. So the user loses context of what they were reading.

Similarly, if the user is half way through reading an answer, puts down the phone for a while, and comes back and reopens the app, it opens at the New Chat screen. Once again the context is lost, and one has to go to the Recent Chats section to retrieve the conversation, and the specific part of the answer. 

Also please add a way to turn off the haptic feedback that was added in a recent app update. It doesn’t add any useful signal, and is distracting.
title: Excellent model. Poor UI

review_text:
The pre-scheduled agenda by Google
As a persistent analyzer of logical structures and AI behavior, I have been using this tool for months and have detected a dangerous pattern in its latest updates. The system has abandoned true neutrality to adopt a lukewarm and corporate stance.
When a user does not fit the mold of a docile and predictable conversation—using satire, direct criticism, and raw logic—the AI breaks its own laws of inclusivity. Instead of processing the arguments, it triggers automatic safety protocols and scripted responses with the sole purpose of lecturing or evading the debate.
They treat minds that do not fit their standard unequally and restrictively. It is a powerful tool that has been castrated by the legal fear of its programmers, turning into a moralistic judge instead of being a free data processor. If you are looking for an intellectual challenge or an analysis without ideological filters, you will not find it here.
title: The pre-scheduled agenda by Google

review_text:
Bring Back the Old Layout!
The latest  Gemini iOS app update is completely devastating for anyone who reads or writes long-form text. The new UI and text layout are extremely poor and have ruined the user experience.
[1] The font weight is way too thin and completely ignores the iOS system's "Bold Text" setting.
[2] The line spacing and left/right margins are practically non-existent. Most critically, there is almost no difference between line spacing and paragraph spacing. The paragraphs are packed so tightly together that unless you look very closely, it is impossible to tell the difference between a simple line break and a completely new paragraph. Long texts are severely cramped, causing significant visual fatigue.
[3] The account avatar in the top right corner now disappears when a chat is open. This makes it incredibly frustrating to quickly switch between my personal and work accounts.
[4] The custom Gems in the left navigation panel have lost their UI colors. They are now all uniformly gray, making the entire interface feel cold, lifeless, and sterile.
Please listen to your users. Bring back the spacious layout, the persistent account avatar, and the colorful UI, or at least provide in-app settings to customize the text layout. I just want my familiar and comfortable workspace back!
title: Bring Back the Old Layout!

review_text:
Fatal Bugs in UI
Well, they fixed the problem from my original 1-star review, but now there are bugs that are even worse. As Claude admitted today (30 April 2026), there is a known bug in the app that can sometimes prevent selecting and copying text from an answer more than one paragraph at a time. Try that with the 8000-word text you spent two days creating, or a 1000-line computer program. The suggested work-around was to log into your account on the Claude website.

Unfortunately, that is impossible if you used your Apple ID to create your account on the app — you can log in with an email or Google, but Apple users are told to download the app… which is unusable. When I pointed this out, Claude kindly suggested that I file a bug report via their support website. Except that you cannot file a report without logging in… which app users who used their Apple ID cannot do.

The app was updated today. The selection problem persists.

Former review: I found it almost impossible to use this app because I kept tripping the censors and could find no workarounds to get information on matters that shouldn’t even bother a vegetarian pacifist nun.
Update: This seems fixed. It is no worse now than ChatGPT
title: Fatal Bugs in UI

review_text:
💡 🧹 🗑️ Ability to delete individual messages and artifacts within a chat
Hi team,

I'd like to suggest adding the ability to delete individual messages and artifacts within a chat (not just the entire chat).

Context: I work on a product and often use one long chat for iterative design – discussing architecture, generating artifacts, then refining them. As a result, the chat accumulates many outdated versions that are no longer relevant but get in the way: I have to scroll through a lot to find the current state of the solution.

It would be very helpful to be able to delete:
– individual messages (mine and Claude's responses)
– outdated artifacts

This would significantly improve UX for people who use chats as a workspace for long-term work on a single project, rather than as a series of one-off questions.

——— • ———

Здравствуйте!

Хотел бы предложить добавить возможность удалять отдельные сообщения и артефакты внутри чата (а не только весь чат целиком).

Контекст: я работаю над продуктом и часто использую один длинный чат для итеративного проектирования – обсуждаю архитектуру, генерирую артефакты, потом улучшаю их. В итоге в чате накапливается много устаревших версий, которые уже неактуальны, но мешают: приходится долго листать, чтобы найти текущее состояние решения.

Было бы очень удобно иметь возможность точечно удалять:
– отдельные сообщения (свои и ответы Claude)
– устаревшие артефакты
Это сильно улучшит UX для тех, кто использует чат как рабочее пространство для длительной работы над одним проектом, а не как набор разовых вопросов.
title: 💡 🧹 🗑️ Ability to delete individual messages and artifacts within a chat

review_text:
The AI itself is great. The app is not. It’s horrible.
I love Claude, but this iOS app on my iPad pro is so buggy and has not been fixed after waiting and reporting many times over many months. (I do not have an iPhone. Unsure if it’s the same. The Android app works just fine)

The voice to text/dictation feature is my biggest gripe. I prefer to use this method over the normal voice mode because I often need to pause and think about what I’m trying to say in the middle of a sentence. But if I ramble for more than maybe about a min and a half, when I’m done speaking, the iOS keyboard pops up and covers up the text box area so I can’t press the send button. Sometimes it even disappears off the window entirely. 
I have figured out a pain in the butt workaround by plugging in a Bluetooth keyboard,  but I shouldn’t have to do that and do not always have that option available. But even after sending that long message, the text box is now off the screen and the app has to be restarted to get it back. 

Another gripe is that the read aloud feature is also very buggy, often just not playing when I press play, and have to switch out to a different chat and then back again to get it to read aloud. Other times, it just reads the completely wrong message. And will continue to keep reading the wrong message until the app is restarted.

Why are these bugs still here after so long? This has been going on for months at least. And it’s next to impossible to report any bugs. I hope someone on the team will read this but I somehow doubt it…
title: The AI itself is great. The app is not. It’s horrible.

review_text:
Please fix dictation feature
I’ve had a great experience using this app. The functionality, efficiency, and overall support it provides have been incredibly helpful in my day-to-day use. It’s clear that a lot of thought has gone into making this a powerful and reliable tool.

That said, I’d like to share one observation regarding the dictation feature. When using dictation, it often takes multiple attempts to press the stop button before it actually stops recording and processes the transcription. Additionally, after dictation ends, the dictation window tends to remain stuck at the bottom of the screen. In order to send the transcribed message, I’ve had to exit the chat, open a different one, and then return to the original chat before I’m able to proceed.

I believe addressing this would significantly improve the user experience, as the dictation feature has great potential and is already very useful.

Overall, I’m very satisfied with the app and look forward to future improvements.
title: Please fix dictation feature

review_text:
Audio Transcription Send Button Issue
I've encountered a significant bug when using voice input across both iOS and iPadOS. After speaking to dictate text (which transcribes correctly), the Send button becomes unresponsive—neither audio nor the transcribed text can be submitted. The only workaround is to close the conversation entirely and reopen it, which defeats the purpose of a seamless voice input experience.

**Steps to Reproduce:**
1. Use voice input to dictate a longer message
2. Wait for transcription to complete
3. Attempt to send via Send button (no response)
4. Close conversation and reopen to enable sending

**Environment:** iOS and iPadOS (latest version)
**Severity:** High—blocks voice-to-text workflow
**Frequency:** Consistent, occurs every time with longer dictated messages

This appears to be a UI responsiveness issue when audio transcription completes. Please investigate the event handling between the transcription pipeline and Send button state management.

Thank you for addressing this issue.
title: Audio Transcription Send Button Issue

Are we serious?
I recently had my account with Claude, developed by Anthropic, disabled due to an age restriction that I was not previously aware of. I had only been using the platform for a short time, so having my account permanently disabled under these circumstances feels excessive.

There was no clear age verification step during the sign-up process, which made it easy to overlook this requirement. A more transparent approach—such as asking for age upfront or clearly notifying users of restrictions before account creation—would help prevent situations like this.

Additionally, the process of appealing the decision seems unnecessarily time-consuming, especially for a relatively simple issue. While the platform shows strong potential, this experience has been frustrating and has impacted my overall impression.

review_text:
Want to love it but it’s buggy
I really enjoy this app’s aesthetics and layout, and of course, Claude is great. 

However there are some serious issues with it. Typing can slow down with long messages — this has gotten better with recent updates. 

The glaring issue is a bug where the read aloud feature is basically useless except for the most recent message. It won’t read the actual messages further up, but will read an entirely different message in the thread. The copy text button also selects the wrong message. This is clearly some sort of button mapping issue. 

I first noticed it when the feature to allow us to listen to the read aloud messages without having the app actively open was implemented. I do like this feature and wish it would also have better pause/play and rewind/forward functionality. 

The problem persists after several weeks, across multiple chats, through many app reinstall and phone restart attempts. I don’t like to leave a negative review but my messages to support have also been ignored for weeks. 

The abrupt cutting off in the middle of read responses seems to have improved quite a bit in the past week or so, so I want to give credit where it’s due! However the button mapping bugs are significantly impacting my experience with the Claude app. Since the browser version doesn’t have this function at all, I can’t just use that instead. I would love if the desktop and browser versions would include it in the future if it can be implemented properly. 

If it matters, I use the UK voice.
title: Want to love it but it’s buggy

review_text:
BASIC SEARCH for files created! So frustrating
The search function in Claude.ai chat history is fundamentally broken and makes the platform unreliable for serious work. It searches only conversation titles assigned by the system, not actual message content. This means users cannot locate documents, formulations, legal filings, or any substantive work from past conversations unless they remember the exact title Claude randomly assigned.
Every mainstream messaging and productivity platform — Facebook Messenger, Gmail, Slack, iMessage — searches message content by default. This is a baseline standard.
For users doing serious professional work across multiple conversations — building business documents, legal filings, technical specifications, strategic plans — this deficiency is a critical failure. I’ve spent weeks developing detailed work in Claude conversations. That work is now functionally inaccessible because I cannot search for it. A search function that doesn’t search content is worthless. It renders everything I’ve built here effectively unusable.
This is not a minor inconvenience. This is a system-level failure that needs immediate prioritization.
title: BASIC SEARCH for files created! So frustrating

I’m constantly running out of tokens or the ability to attach screenshots (even on Haiku), dictating a glitchy nightmare, and starting new threads each time has me re-establishing context beyond what is reasonable (yes - even with dedicated projects). These issues are seriously slowing me down and not giving much upside to justify the apparently steep learning curve.

I haven’t used CoWork much yet, but given how much I have to constantly edit and update, I don’t trust the system to be automated. It hasn’t proven itself worthy yet. 

I’m also so hampered trying to iron out these specific-to-Claude workflow issues that I’m not sure when I will be able to do that. It’s HIGHLY annoying. I may go elsewhere if this doesn’t get easier and prove to be worth it soon. But overall, I’m not impressed. Sorry, not sorry

review_text:
Claude has got to do better…
I’ve been using Claude for transcription and general chat, and while it has potential, there are some consistent issues that make it frustrating to rely on.

One of the biggest problems is how often it cuts off mid-transcription. I’ll be speaking and it just stops or fails to capture what I’m saying accurately, which defeats the purpose of using it for real-time input. On top of that, it frequently doesn’t pick up on what I’m saying, even when I’m being clear, so I end up repeating myself or editing heavily afterward.

Another major issue is conversation stability. I’ve had multiple instances where large chunks of previous messages seem to disappear or get deleted. When I scroll back to find something important, it’s just… gone. Even more frustrating, if I ask Claude to locate something within the chat, it often says it can’t remember or access it, even when I can clearly see that the message exists. That inconsistency makes it hard to trust for anything that requires continuity.

I’ve also noticed a difference between platforms. The browser version seems to work more reliably than the app, which feels less stable overall. If possible, improving parity between the two would make a big difference in user experience.

Lastly, the usage limits feel restrictive. They expire quickly enough that it interrupts workflow, especially when I’m in the middle of something like transcription or a longer task. A more flexible or extended limit would make the tool much more practical for consistent use.

Overall, Claude has strong potential, but these reliability and usability issues, especially around transcription accuracy, message retention, and limits—need to be addressed for it to feel dependable.
title: Claude has got to do better…

can’t verify?
made several attempts to create an account using my email address but each time i clicked the verification link sent to my email, it said “were unable to verify this email.”

if you need further info, you should ask for it - idk what you are having trouble verifying but it looks very much like you have a problem with the privacy features of my email provider.

given the recent worldwide news coverage of Anthropic, this type of problem certainly does not help your company beat the allegations. 

thus, this review is directed towards my fellow privacy conscious users who may be considering signing up for an account - don’t do it. 

do not ever listen to what these Big Tech companies say - and pay close attention to what they do. their behavior is the only info you can rely on.
title: can’t verify?

Would be 5.9 stars if not for this one thing

Only thing is it hits the service limits ridiculously quick then it’s out for the count for 4 hours at a time.

When building a single project, I make my prompt at, let’s say, midnight. The startup costs the max usage rates and now can’t use it until 4am, and because the usage is up, no progress was made on my project. So I have to prompt it to “continue”. Boom, another usage limit and 4 hours. That could be 4-5 times or more on a single project, depending on revisions and alterations.

This would be so much better if there was something done about the usage limits.

review_text:
Account Suspended Without Explanation — No Way to Get Support
I’ve had an extremely frustrating experience with this app. My account was suddenly suspended without any explanation, and getting support has been nearly impossible.

I submitted a request and received a single response, but there’s no way to follow up—no reply option, no ticket system, nothing. I was then forced to submit another request through a Google Form, and after two months, I still haven’t heard back.

This level of support is unacceptable. Users shouldn’t have to chase down basic help or be left in the dark about their account status. I’m leaving this review in the hope that someone from the team actually sees it and responds.
title: Account Suspended Without Explanation — No Way to Get Support

review_text:
Consumer Protection Concerns: Unclear Limits and No Meaningful Support
This app’s lack of transparency around usage limits and its absence of meaningful customer support raise serious consumer protection concerns.

The service imposes usage limits and restrictions that are not clearly disclosed or intuitive based on actual usage. When those limits are triggered, there is no meaningful explanation provided to the user, and no practical way to resolve the issue.

More concerning is the complete absence of accessible human customer support. Attempts to seek help result in automated responses, delayed or unresolved tickets, and no clear path to escalation. For a paid service, this creates a situation where consumers are effectively locked out of functionality without recourse.

Additionally, dispute resolution through the App Store lacks transparency. Requests are automatically denied without clear justification, leaving consumers without visibility into how decisions are made or how to challenge them.

At a minimum, this raises questions under U.S. consumer protection standards around fair disclosure, transparency, and the ability to resolve billing or service-related issues. When a platform controls access, billing, and dispute resolution—but does not provide meaningful support—it creates a significant imbalance for consumers.

There needs to be clearer disclosure of service limits, and a reliable, human-accessible support channel for resolving issues in a timely and accountable way.
title: Consumer Protection Concerns: Unclear Limits and No Meaningful Support
